tool.name: Protein Calculator

regex.description: Protein sequence contains invalid character(s). Please enter a valid Protein Sequence.
DNA sequence detected. Please enter a valid Protein Sequence.
Protein sequence should have a minimum length of 10 residues. Please enter a valid Protein Sequence.
Protein sequence should have a maximum length of 3000 residues. Please enter a valid Protein Sequence.
Failed to fetch Galaxy BE version. Please try again.

tool.command:
cp -r '$__tool_directory__/templates' . && python '$__tool_directory__/protein_calculator.py' #if $name: --name '$name' #end if
#if $sequence: --sequence '$sequence' #else: echo 'Error: Protein sequence is required.' >&2 exit 1 #end if

param.name: input_mode
param.label: FASTA Input
param.help: Protein sequence(s) to calculate protein properties of. Input can be a FASTA file from history, or text.

param.label: Protein name
param.help: Name of the protein (optional if FASTA has headers).

param.label: Fasta file from your current history
param.help: Select a FASTA protein file containing one or more protein sequences from your history. Name of the output files is derived from first word of the sequence title.

param.label: Protein sequence
param.help: Sequence to be processed. Protein sequence should have a minimum length of 10 residues.

tool.help:
This Python-based tool will determine protein properties based on the amino acid sequence, which it summarizes in an HTML report and Plotly graph.
.. class:: warningmark
The maximum allowed sequence is set at 3.000 amino acids.
.. class:: infomark
Usage:
- Raw sequence: ``protein_calculator.py [-h] --name NAME --sequence SEQUENCE``
- FASTA input file: ``protein_calculator.py [-h] [--name NAME] --fasta /path/to/file.fasta``
Arguments:
-h, --help Show this help message and exit
--name Name of the protein, in text format. When using a FASTA file, this argument is optional as the name will be derived from the FASTA header and will be ignored when the FASTA contains multiple sequences
--sequence Sequence to be processed, in text format
--fasta Location of the .fasta file, in FASTA format
-v, --version Show the version of the tool and exit

citations.citation: @misc{githubProtein_Calculator, author = {Balcaen, Kevin and Fonteyn, Robbe}, year = {2025}, title = {Protein_Calculator}, publisher = {GitHub}, journal = {GitHub repository}, url = {https://github.com/KBalcaen/Protein_Calculator}, }
